subsystem: registers_graphs (SHAPE lane: mechanics_index.yaml, canonical_sources.yaml, supersession_register.yaml, tools/observability/DECISIONS.md, tests/coverage_matrix.md)
core_loop: Not a player loop — this lane audits corpus SHAPE artifacts (mechanics_index.yaml, canonical_sources.yaml, supersession_register.yaml, DECISIONS.md, coverage_matrix.md), the meta-layer that is supposed to tell designers/agents what's currently canonical and tested. Its "loop" is: author mechanic → canon doc → registry entry → (ideally) re-sync on change. That last step is the broken link this dossier documents.

decision_points.options: not_implemented / provisional / validated_n* / canonical / superseded — a designer's read of 'does X exist'
decision_points.note: Field is stale for at least 2 of the most active subsystems (mass_battle, social_contest); a reader cannot use it to decide anything reliably.

decision_points.options: P1 (blocking) vs P2/P3 — meant to let a designer triage 957 open items
decision_points.note: 53% tagged P1; dominated by cosmetic naming collisions, so the tag barely discriminates real blockers from debt.

decision_points.where: canonical_sources.yaml SHA pins vs sim/ ratified changes
decision_points.options: whether a ratified sim-level rule change gets back-written into the canon-doc pin
decision_points.note: Currently a one-way gate with no enforced return path — mass_battle's entire re-architecture never round-tripped.

north_star.collision_feed: This territory feeds the collision engine nothing directly (no player-facing state) — its job is to keep the OTHER lanes' currency claims trustworthy. When it drifts, downstream subsystems' choice-richness/legibility claims become unverifiable, which indirectly starves the collision engine of confidence rather than content.
north_star.verdict: Points away from the North Star by omission: the registries meant to keep every subsystem's currency legible are themselves stale exactly where the corpus is most alive right now (mass_battle), undermining the audit's own method discipline (establish currency via registries first).

nq.observation: DECISIONS.md's P1/P2/P3 severity split fails to discriminate — 507/957 items P1, dominated by cosmetic naming-collision flags — so the instrument doesn't actually smooth a designer's triage path.

mechanics_index.yaml's test_status is not robust to subsystem velocity: last regenerated 2026-05-17, and the one entry (combat) hand-patched since then carries no marker distinguishing it from the ~59 stale ones.

Two registries with overlapping scope (mechanics_index.yaml mechanic-list vs module_contracts.yaml module-list) use divergent schemas (one has no orphan/staleness field, the other does) and never cross-reference — a corpus-shape inelegance.

threadwork_junctures.evidence: canon/mechanics_index.yaml L356-464 — structurally accounted for as a first-class scale, but every entry test_status: not_implemented, and the index carries no field for cross-scale P-14 inseparability (that lives only in scale_transitions_v30.md, uncited here).

threadwork_junctures.juncture: supersession_register.yaml tracking a thread-mechanic supersession event
threadwork_junctures.evidence: ED-912 (2026-06-28, Knot/Disposition redesign) IS present in supersession_register.yaml — the register does track threadwork-adjacent supersessions when authored; the gap this dossier found is scope (mass_battle), not a threadwork-specific absence.

legibility.note: These are meta/tooling artifacts with no in-fiction actors, stakes, or seasons — the three dramatic-legibility questions don't apply to this lane's subject matter.

degenerate_candidates: DECISIONS.md P1-inflation: 507/957 items tagged 'decide first' — tools/observability/DECISIONS.md:5 — when everything is P1 nothing is, a governance-triage degeneracy rather than a gameplay one.

mechanics_index.yaml as a trust source: zero enforcement gate requires regeneration on subsystem change, so its 'current state' claim can drift arbitrarily without CI/workflow consequence — canon/mechanics_index.yaml:11-14.

edges.mechanism: executable ratifications (ED-1089..1096, ED-MB-0001/0002) should flow back into canonical_sources.yaml SHA pins + supersession_register.yaml
hand-patch of a single mechanics_index.yaml entry without bumping file-level last_authored/drift_report
both claim to index 'what mechanics/modules exist'; no cross-reference field in either direction
mechanics_index social_contest entry should reflect rebuild progress

directions.top-down: P-14 (inseparability everywhere) has no corresponding compliance field in mechanics_index.yaml or module_contracts.yaml — the philosophy has no shape-layer instrument checking it.
directions.bottom-up: Executable sim work (coverage_matrix.md's mass_battle log) has raced far ahead of the design-index layer meant to describe it, inverting the normal top-down authoring flow CLAUDE.md describes.
directions.lateral: Sibling registries (mechanics_index, module_contracts, canonical_sources, supersession_register, DECISIONS.md) don't cross-reference each other's staleness signals, so a reader must manually reconcile four documents to get one honest currency picture.
directions.diagonal: Sim-scale (code) ratifications never round-trip to canon-doc-scale (prose + SHA pins) — a diagonal drift class distinct from any single-scale problem.
directions.forwards: No workplan/queue item currently tracks 'regenerate mechanics_index.yaml' or 'back-propagate mass_battle ratifications to canon' as pending work — it's invisible to forward planning.
directions.backwards: A session trusting mechanics_index.yaml's mass_battle or social_contest entries would resume work from a materially false 'not built yet' baseline — exactly the resumed-from-superseded risk the charter warns against.

findings.claim: mechanics_index.yaml's mass_battle entry (test_status: not_implemented, '<5% of v22 feature surface') is falsified by tests/coverage_matrix.md's 3-week log of a mature, byte-exact-tested, Jordan-ratified engine (rally/rout/morale-pull, multi-unit orchestrator, envelopment/refused-flank compositions, TOI physics).

None of the ratified mass-battle default-path/rule changes since 2026-06-30 (ED-1089..1096, ED-MB-0001/0002 — FIELD_MOVEMENT/PER_CELL default flips, subunit cap, recoil gating, DG-3/DG-4 pool math) are reflected in canonical_sources.yaml's SHA pins or logged in supersession_register.yaml, despite being supersession-shaped (which resolution path is canonically default changed).

The registry's combat entry was hand-patched post-generation (cites 2026-06-23/ED-1041) while file-level last_authored and drift_report.last_generation both still read 2026-05-17 with no marker distinguishing refreshed vs frozen entries.
social_contest entry ('v17 missing entirely, degenerate single-roll only') contradicts the charter's own calibration note that Stages 0-3 of the social-contest rebuild are done, Stage 4 next.

53% of all 957 open items are tagged P1, dominated by cosmetic naming-collision flags rather than load-bearing decisions, so the severity tag fails to triage — a designer cannot use 'is this P1' to decide what to work on next.

The file's own title ('Weapon System v2 (Active)') no longer describes its content, which is entirely mass_battle engine coverage from 2026-06-15 onward — a shape artifact whose header has drifted from what it tracks.

Two registries with overlapping scope (mechanic-list vs module-list) use divergent schemas — module_contracts.yaml tracks doc:null/[ASSUMPTION]/orphan status, mechanics_index.yaml has no equivalent staleness/orphan field at all — and neither cross-references the other.
findings.cite: canon/mechanics_index.yaml (categories section, no gap-tracking field) vs designs/audit/2026-07-04-ners-qualitative-audit/00_grounding/02_interdependency_map.md §(b)
